Argument.Name: path
Argument.Description: Path to the collection, test case (.http) or single-file test case (.tp) which will be tested. Defaults to the current directory.

Option.Description: Displays debug information. Default: false.

Path to file, which contains definitions of available environments. If not provided, the tool will use the first matching 'env.json' file found in '.teapie' folder or the collection (respectively parent folder of test case).
Name of the environment on which application will be run.

Path to script, which will be used for initialization before the first test-case execution. If not provided, the tool will use the first matching 'init.csx' file found in '.teapie' folder or the collection (respectively parent folder of test case).
Path to the file where all logs will be saved.
Log level for the log file (if the log file path is specified). Supported levels: Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical, None.
Log level for console output. Supported levels: Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical, None.
Disables loading variables from file and caching them to file.
Runs command silently, without displaying any output. Default: false.
Path to file, which will be used for test results summary report generation. If this option is not used, no report to file is generated.
Path to the file where structured JSON data about HTTP requests will be saved.
Temporary path for the application. Defaults to the system's temporary folder with a TeaPie sub-folder if no path is provided.
Displays all available information, including debug details. Default: false.

Argument.Name: path
Argument.Description: Path at which .teapie folder should be created. If not specified, it attempts to find root of repository (the one containing '.git' folder).

Option.Description: Displays debug information. Default: false.
Path to the file where all logs will be saved.
Log level for the log file (if the log file path is specified). Supported levels: Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical, None.
Log level for console output. Supported levels: Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical, None.
Runs command silently, without displaying any output. Default: false.
Path to the file where structured JSON data about HTTP requests will be saved.
Displays all available information, including debug details. Default: false.

Command.Name: test
Command.Description: Runs tests from the collection at the specified path. If no path is provided, the current directory is used.

Argument.Name: path
Argument.Description: Path to the collection, test case (.http) or single-file test case (.tp) which will be tested. Defaults to the current directory.

Option.Description: Displays debug information. Default: false.

Path to file, which contains definitions of available environments. If not provided, the tool will use the first matching 'env.json' file found in '.teapie' folder or the collection (respectively parent folder of test case).
Name of the environment on which application will be run.

Path to script, which will be used for initialization before the first test-case execution. If not provided, the tool will use the first matching 'init.csx' file found in '.teapie' folder or the collection (respectively parent folder of test case).
Path to the file where all logs will be saved.
Log level for the log file (if the log file path is specified). Supported levels: Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical, None.
Log level for console output. Supported levels: Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical, None.
Disables loading variables from file and caching them to file.
Runs command silently, without displaying any output. Default: false.
Path to file, which will be used for test results summary report generation. If this option is not used, no report to file is generated.
Path to the file where structured JSON data about HTTP requests will be saved.
Temporary path for the application. Defaults to the system's temporary folder with a TeaPie sub-folder if no path is provided.
Displays all available information, including debug details. Default: false.

Argument.Name: name
Argument.Description: Name of test case to be generated.

Argument.Name: path
Argument.Description: Path at which test case will be created.

Option.Description: Indicates whether to generate pre-request script (with '-init' suffix).
Generate a single-file test case (.tp) instead of multi-file format.
Indicates whether to generate post-response script (with '-test' suffix).

Command.Name: explore
Command.Description: Explores collection structure and displays it.

Argument.Name: path
Argument.Description: Path to collection, test case (.http) or single-file test case (.tp) which will be explored. Defaults to the current directory.

Option.Description: Displays debug information. Default: false.

Path to file, which contains definitions of available environments. If this option is not used, first found file within '.teapie' folder or collection (respectively parent folder of test case) with name 'env.json' is used.

Path to script, which will be used for initialization before the first test-case execution. If this option is not used, first found file within '.teapie' folder or collection (respectively parent folder of test case) with name 'init.csx' is used.
Path to the file where all logs will be saved.
Log level for the log file (if the log file path is specified). Supported levels: Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical, None.
Log level for console output. Supported levels: Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical, None.
Runs command silently, without displaying any output. Default: false.
Path to the file where structured JSON data about HTTP requests will be saved.
Displays all available information, including debug details. Default: false.

Command.Name: compile
Command.Description: Attempts to compile script at specified path.

Argument.Name: path
Argument.Description: Path to script (.csx) or test case file (.tp) which should be compiled.

Option.Description: Displays debug information. Default: false.
Path to the file where all logs will be saved.
Log level for the log file (if the log file path is specified). Supported levels: Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical, None.
Log level for console output. Supported levels: Trace, Debug, Information, Warning, Error, Critical, None.
Runs command silently, without displaying any output. Default: false.
Path to the file where structured JSON data about HTTP requests will be saved.
Displays all available information, including debug details. Default: false.
Indicates whether to clear global cache (in system temporary folder).
Indicates whether to clear global cache (in system temporary folder).
